ItacPersonItem.Notes: Monticulo was a renowned jurist. He received his doctoral degree from the University of Padua, where in 1562 he started teaching criminal law. As from 1565 he taught Justinian law, and from 1577 he passed to teach Canon law. He joined the Ricovrati in 1602. In the Academy, Monticulo and Galilei were `censori sopra le stampe`.
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
